name: Marbles
description: Random sampler with master clock. One t-section emits probabilistic gates, one x-section emits quantized random voltages. A deja-vu knob locks patterns and a scale learner stores up to six custom scales.

controls.name: Rate (t)
controls.description: Master clock rate. Drives the three T1/T2/T3 gate outputs. Overridden by external clock when T CLOCK patched.
controls.detail: ~0.5 Hz to ~100 Hz · bipolar attenuverter for T RATE CV

controls.name: Bias (t)
controls.description: Sets gate distribution between T1 and T3. CCW = only T1, CW = only T3, noon = balanced. T2 always fires as coin-flip between them.

controls.name: Jitter (t)
controls.description: Random clock jitter / tempo instability. CCW = locked grid, CW = progressively more timing drift up to fully random.

controls.name: t Mode
controls.description: Cycles Bernoulli (coin flip on each tick) · clusters (sparse/dense bursts) · divider (harmonic-ratio division of master clock).
controls.detail: Three LEDs indicate active mode · affects how T1/T2/T3 interrelate

controls.name: Deja Vu
controls.description: Pattern-locking probability. CCW = always fresh random. Noon = 50% probability a past step repeats. CW = full loop of length set by LENGTH.
controls.detail: Shared t & x by default · separate sections via t/x Deja Vu buttons

controls.name: Length (Deja Vu)
controls.description: Loop length when Deja Vu is active. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16 steps selectable.
controls.detail: LED row shows current length · hold Length button + turn knob to edit

controls.name: Rate (x)
controls.description: X sample rate. Sets how often X1/X2/X3 redraw new voltages. Normalized to t clock but can be set independently.
controls.detail: External X CLOCK overrides · bipolar CV attenuverter

controls.name: Spread (x)
controls.description: Width of the random distribution around center. CCW = narrow/centered, CW = wide.

controls.name: Bias (x)
controls.description: Shifts center of the random distribution. Moves the average voltage up or down.

controls.name: Steps (x)
controls.description: Discrete-step count / quantization of the x output. Interacts with scale learner. Noon = continuous.
controls.detail: CW adds steps up to full scale quantization · CCW smooths toward continuous

controls.name: x Mode
controls.description: Cycles identical-independent-independent · 3 voices of 1 octave · shift-register (voltages chain down X1→X2→X3).
controls.detail: Shift-register mode produces melodic-in-motion relationships between voices

controls.name: t Deja Vu / x Deja Vu buttons
controls.description: Independent enable/disable of Deja Vu for the t and x sections. Lets timing loop while CV stays free, or vice-versa.

inputs.name: T CLOCK
inputs.description: External clock for t section. Replaces internal oscillator.

inputs.name: X CLOCK
inputs.description: External clock for x section. Falls back to T CLOCK (or internal if none).

inputs.name: X STEPS CV
inputs.description: CV for step count / quantization depth.

inputs.name: DEJA VU CV
inputs.description: CV for deja-vu probability (global for t and x).

outputs.name: T1 / T2 / T3
outputs.description: Three gate outputs. Distribution depends on t mode and bias. T2 is always a coin-flip between T1 and T3.

outputs.name: X1 / X2 / X3
outputs.description: Three random CV outputs. Range, spread, and quantization set by x controls.

outputs.name: Y
outputs.description: Fourth random CV output. Slow noise-like voltage, 16× slower than X by default, quantized by the same scale.

behaviors.name: t Bernoulli mode
behaviors.description: Each tick of the master clock independently chooses T1 or T3 based on bias. T2 fires on every clock tick. Classic probabilistic rhythm generator.

behaviors.name: t Clusters mode
behaviors.description: Gates emitted in bursts (dense groups) separated by silences. Bias sets cluster density; jitter adds timing variance. Good for Berlin-school and generative rhythmic bursts.

behaviors.name: t Divider mode
behaviors.description: T1/T2/T3 output harmonic ratios of master clock — bias selects the ratio. Works like a polyrhythmic clock divider with smooth bias-sweepable cross-fading.

behaviors.name: x Shift-register mode
behaviors.description: X1, X2, X3 behave as a 3-stage shift register: each clock shifts X1→X2, X2→X3; X1 draws a new value. Produces relationships where X2 and X3 are echoes of X1.

behaviors.name: Deja Vu locking
behaviors.description: At full CW the sequence loops exactly. Below CW, each step has a probability of being replaced by a new random value — so the loop slowly mutates. Can lock t and x independently via the sub-buttons.

behaviors.name: Custom scale learner
behaviors.description: Marbles learns up to six user scales from incoming V/OCT (press+hold scale button while playing notes). Slot 1 is chromatic, slots 2–6 are user-programmable.

behaviors.name: Y slow companion
behaviors.description: Y emits a random voltage that updates 16× slower than X by default, but shares the same scale quantization. Useful for slow transposition offsets or timbral drift alongside a fast melody on X.

behaviors.name: Internal vs external clocking
behaviors.description: Patching T CLOCK replaces the internal oscillator. The internal oscillator is high-quality (sub-ms jitter) and syncable to modular-standard clocks.

behaviors.name: X-clock fallback to T
behaviors.description: With no X CLOCK cable, X updates at the same rate as T, so x-rate knob acts as a divider/multiplier relative to T. Patching X CLOCK lets x and t run at entirely independent rates.

behaviors.name: Deja Vu pattern mutation
behaviors.description: Between half-CW and full-CW the locked pattern has a non-zero chance of swapping one step per cycle. Produces the 'Music for Airports' slowly-evolving motif effect.

patchIdeas.name: Locked generative melody
patchIdeas.patch: Learn a major scale into slot 2 · X1 → VCO V/OCT · T1 → envelope trigger · Deja Vu full CW, Length 8 · Steps ~2 o'clock · repeatable 8-note melody that can be broken by easing Deja Vu CCW
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* Scale: user slot 2 (major)
* Deja Vu: full CW
* Length: 8

- Marbles (X1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (T1) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bernoulli drum split
patchIdeas.patch: t Mode Bernoulli · Bias at noon (50/50) · T1 → kick trigger · T3 → snare trigger · T2 → hat trigger · Jitter CV from slow LFO for feel · Deja Vu off for endless mutation or CW for locked pattern
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* t Mode: Bernoulli
* t Bias: noon

- LFO (Slow) >> Marbles (T JITTER CV)
- Marbles (T1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Marbles (T2) t> Hat (Trig)
- Marbles (T3) t> Snare (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Shift-register harmonized voices
patchIdeas.patch: x Mode shift-register · learn minor scale · X1 → lead VCO · X2 → harmony VCO (echoes lead with 1-step delay) · X3 → bass VCO (2-step delay) · T2 triggers all three envelopes for simultaneous chord voicing
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* x Mode: Shift Register
* Scale: minor

- Marbles (X1) p> Lead VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (X2) p> Harmony VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (X3) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (T2) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Mixer VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Slow Y transposition
patchIdeas.patch: X1 → main melody VCO · Y → precision adder to add transposition CV over X1 · Y updates slowly (16× slower) · same scale quantization ensures in-key transposition · macro-level key changes every 16 notes
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
- Marbles (X1) p> Adder (In 1)
- Marbles (Y) p> Adder (In 2) // slow transposition
- Adder (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (T1) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Clusters mode for Berlin rhythm
patchIdeas.patch: t Mode Clusters · Bias ~2 o'clock (denser bursts) · T1 → kick, T3 → tom fills, T2 → clock to sequencer · produces flurried bursts of notes separated by rests · Jitter low for tight feel
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* t Mode: Clusters
* t Bias: ~2 o'clock
* t Jitter: low

- Marbles (T1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Marbles (T2) c> Sequencer (Clock)
- Marbles (T3) t> Tom (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Quantized random bass + lead
patchIdeas.patch: Learn scale · x Mode independent · X1 → bass VCO (set Bias CCW for low range) · X3 → lead VCO (Bias CW for high range) · T1 → bass env, T3 → lead env · Deja Vu at 75% for slowly-mutating ostinato
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* Scale: user slot
* x Mode: Independent
* Deja Vu: 75%

- Marbles (X1) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (X3) p> Lead VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (T1) t> Bass Env (Trig)
- Marbles (T3) t> Lead Env (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Divider-mode polyrhythm clock
patchIdeas.patch: t Mode Divider · master clock into T CLOCK · T1/T2/T3 output different ratios selectable by Bias · feeds three independent sequencers running polyrhythmically in lock · Jitter at zero for pristine timing
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* t Mode: Divider
* t Jitter: 0

- Master Clock (Out) c> Marbles (T CLOCK)
- Marbles (T1) c> Sequencer 1 (Clock)
- Marbles (T2) c> Sequencer 2 (Clock)
- Marbles (T3) c> Sequencer 3 (Clock)

patchIdeas.name: External clock, independent x rate
patchIdeas.patch: Modular master clock → T CLOCK · separate fast clock → X CLOCK (say, 8× faster) · x draws new values 8× per beat, feeding a quantized VCO for rapid arpeggios over a slow t-section rhythm
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
- Master Clock (Out) c> Marbles (T CLOCK)
- Fast Clock (Out) c> Marbles (X CLOCK)
- Marbles (T1) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Marbles (X1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
